results.title: Beauty and the beast (Disney)
results.creator_credit: Gary Trousdale, Kirk Wise, Don Hahn, Silver Screen Partners IV and Walt Disney Pictures
results.brief_description: This animated “tale as old as time” follows the adventures of Belle, a bright young woman imprisoned in the castle of a mysterious beast. With the assistance of the castle’s enchanted staff, a tender romance develops between these two unlikely friends and Belle learns the most important lesson of all, that true beauty comes from within. Voices include Angela Lansbsury, Robby Benson and David Ogden Stiers. (Awards: Oscar winning music: Best Song and Best Score).
results.creator_credit_for_label: Gary Trousdale, Kirk Wise, Don Hahn, Silver Screen Partners IV and Walt Disney Pictures

results.title: Beauty and the beast: the enchanted christmas
results.creator_credit: Andy Knight and Walt Disney Television Animation
results.brief_description: Belle vows to bring a little of the Christmas spirit into the Beast’s castle, even though he has lots of misgivings about the success of her plans. All the enchanted objects chip in, even the reluctant Angelique, a beautiful tree ornament. The evil Forte, a pipe organ wants to undo all of Belle’s plans and so spoil Christmas for everyone, but as in all good fairy tales, it all ends up happily ever after with the Christmas spirit intact.

results.title: Beautiful dreamers
results.creator_credit: John Kent Harrison, Martin Walters, Michael Maclear, National Film Board of Canada and Starway Films
results.brief_description: Based on historical events, this film is set in London, Canada in 1880 where Dr Maurice Bucke (Colin Feore) is the new Superintendant at the London Asylum for the Insane. Dr Bucke, a sensitive and humane man, is confronted on a daily basis with the barbaric treatment which his medical staff use to restrain the patients at the Institution - confinement, lobotomies, shock treatment and hysterectomies. At a conference in Philadelphia Bucke meets up with one of America’s greatest writers and poets, Walt Whitman (Rip Torn), who is renowned for his avant-garde ideas about love and sexuality and understanding of the mental condition. At Bucke’s invitation, Walt Whitman visits the London Asylum and before long, the two men embark on a journey of reform - a journey that will radically change Bucke’s life forever, with his wife and with his patients. This is a story of a truly remarkable partnership - of passion, kindness and understanding overcoming prejudice, malpractice and misunderstanding of those who suffer from the unfortunate consequences of mental illness. “By 1882 Dr Bucke had removed all forms of restraint used in the London Asylum. A year later he published ‘Walt Whitman, the man’.” Cast also includes Wendel Meldrum, Sheila McCarthy and Colin Fox.

results.title: Beaches (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: Garry Marshall, Bette Midler, Bonnie Bruckheimer, Margaret Jennings South, Bruckheimer, Silver Screen Partners IV, South and Touchstone Pictures
results.brief_description: The chance meeting of two young girls from totally different backgrounds becomes the friendship of a lifetime. As they lead their separate lives, they come to realise and later rely on the strength that their friendship provides. Cast includes Bette Midler, Barbara Hershey and John Heard.

results.title: Bears and man
results.creator_credit: Bill Schmalz, National Film Board of Canada and Wild Cine Productions
results.brief_description: Two-thirds of all injuries inflicted by bears are inflicted by those who have become panhandling garbage junkies. This film describes the Parks Canada bear-management program in Canada’s mountain national parks. It is designed to protect the visiting public from confrontation with and injury by the bears. Awards: New York; Missoula; Columbus.

results.title: Be clean and healthy
results.creator_credit: Langton Gould-Marks and Australian Instructional Films
results.brief_description: Contrasts healthy children at play with a boy who is “out of everything” because of ill health. Shows simple rules for the care of the body, including the hair and teeth, how to dress comfortably, what to eat, and how to care for eating utensils.

results.title: Baxter earns his wings
results.creator_credit: Don Arioli, Roman Kroitor, Rosemarie Shapley, Wolf Koenig and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Taking the roles of different characters, two actors perform the life story of a person called Baxter. The various significant stages of Baxter’s life, his death and his acceptance into heaven are performed. This delightful production has no dialogue but is accompanied by the actors’ own sound effects. Patrice Arbour stars as Baxter and Bernard Carez as all other characters.

results.title: Bauhaus: face of the twentieth century
results.creator_credit: Julia Cave, BBC and RM Arts
results.brief_description: The development, key figures involved and the historical, political and economic context of the Bauhaus school of architecture, design, and craftsmanship is detailed in this program with narration by Frank Whitford. It is a comprehensive historical and analytical study of the movement which was a turning point in European and then world-wide thought on varied facets of design through its integration of notions of design, industry and functionality. This relationship created designs, buildings, art works and performance art synonymous with rationalised, sharp-edged and industry machine based ideals, which are closely analysed in this program from its development in the early 1900s to the struggles of the revolutionary aims of those involved during the Nazi regime and its contemporary influence. It presents a collection of archival footage, interviews with relevant experts as well as its students and teachers and the representation and discussion of an array of exemplary works and achieves a thorough outline of the Bauhaus school and its impact on modern design and architecture.
